prompt:
-- bench.rail — Benchmark suite for Rail models
-- Runs fixed tasks against a model, scores pass/fail, reports results.
-- Usage: cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native run flywheel/bench.rail [--port 8080]
--
-- The benchmark is deterministic: same tasks every time, comparable across models.
completion:
cat parts = join "" parts
trim s = head (split "\n" s)
q = "\""
json_esc_bs s = join "\\\\" (split "\\" s)
json_esc_q s = join "\\\"" (split "\"" s)
json_esc_nl s = join "\\n" (split "\n" s)
json_esc s = json_esc_nl (json_esc_q (json_esc_bs s))
llm_call port sys_msg user_msg =
prelude _ =
strip_fences code =
join "" parts
join "\\\\" (split "\\" s)
join "\\\"" (split "\"" s)
join "\\n" (split "\n" s)
json_esc_nl (json_esc_q (json_esc_bs s))

prompt:
-- LLM interface - native builtin, parameterized by port
llm_call port sys_msg user_msg =
completion: llm (show port) sys_msg user_msg

prompt:
-- Prelude — helper functions prepended to every generated program
-- The model naturally uses these; defining them avoids "undefined" errors.
prelude _ =
completion:
"-- prelude
starts_with prefix s =
  if length prefix > length s then false
  else starts_with_acc (chars prefix) (chars s)
starts_with_acc pcs scs =
  if length pcs == 0 then true
  else if head pcs == head scs then starts_with_acc (tail pcs) (tail scs)
  else false
flatten xss =
  if length xss == 0 then []
  else append (head xss) (flatten (tail xss))
find_line xs prefix =
  if length xs == 0 then ""
  else if str_contains prefix (head xs) then head xs
  else find_line (tail xs) prefix
count_matches xs s =
  if length xs == 0 then 0
  else if str_contains s (head xs) then 1 + count_matches (tail xs) s
  else count_matches (tail xs) s
parse_int s = parse_int_acc (chars s) 0
parse_int_acc cs acc =
  if length cs == 0 then acc
  else parse_int_acc (tail cs) (acc * 10 + digit_val (head cs))
digit_val c = if c == "0" then 0 else if c == "1" then 1 else if c == "2" then 2 else if c == "3" then 3 else if c == "4" then 4 else if c == "5" then 5 else if c == "6" then 6 else if c == "7" then 7 else if c == "8" then 8 else if c == "9" then 9 else 0
rev_word w = join "" (reverse (chars w))
insert x xs =
  if length xs == 0 then [x]
  else if x <= head xs then cons x xs
  else cons (head xs) (insert x (tail xs))
isort xs =
  if length xs == 0 then []
  else insert (head xs) (isort (tail xs))

"

prompt:
-- Strip markdown code fences if present
strip_fences code =
completion:
if str_contains "```" code then
    let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_fence_raw.txt" code
    let _ = shell "perl -0777 -pi -e 's/```[a-z]*\\n?//g' /tmp/rail_fence_raw.txt"
    read_file "/tmp/rail_fence_raw.txt"
  else code

prompt:
-- Grammar pre-check: catch syntax errors before the compiler (fast, targeted hints)
grammar_precheck file =
completion: trim (shell (cat ["python3 tools/train/grammar_check.py ", file, " --hint 2>/dev/null"]))

prompt:
-- Verify with optional expected output (empty = compile+run only)
-- Grammar pre-check runs alongside compiler (choice 1B: richer signal, both hints combined)
verify code expected =
completion:
let clean = strip_fences code
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_bench_test.rail" clean
  -- Grammar pre-check (if grammar_check.py exists) — saved for retry hint, does NOT block compile
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_bench_grammar_hint.txt" (grammar_precheck "/tmp/rail_bench_test.rail")
  let _ = shell "rm -f /tmp/rail_bench_bin"
  let compile_out = shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native /tmp/rail_bench_test.rail 2>&1 && cp /tmp/rail_out /tmp/rail_bench_bin 2>&1"
  let check = trim (shell "test -f /tmp/rail_bench_bin && echo ok || echo fail")
  if check == "fail" then
    -- Combine grammar hint with compiler error for richer retry signal
    let ghint = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_bench_grammar_hint.txt 2>/dev/null")
    if length ghint > 5 then cat ["syntax_error:", ghint]
    else "compile_fail"
  else
    let _ = shell "perl -e 'alarm 5; exec @ARGV' /tmp/rail_bench_bin > /tmp/rail_bench_out.txt 2>&1; echo $? > /tmp/rail_bench_exit.txt"
    let actual = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_bench_out.txt 2>/dev/null")
    let exit_code = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_bench_exit.txt 2>/dev/null")
    if exit_code != "0" then
      if exit_code == "142" then "timeout"
      else "runtime_fail"
    else if expected == "" then
      if actual != "" then "success" else "runtime_fail"
    else
      if actual == expected then "success"
      else "wrong_output"

if length xs == 0 then ""
  else if length xs == 1 then head xs
  else
    let last = last_nonempty (tail xs)
    if last == "" then head xs else last

prompt:
-- Few-shot retrieval: find closest training examples for a task
-- Writes query to temp file to avoid shell quoting issues
retrieve_examples task =
completion:
let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_bench_query.txt" task
  trim (shell "python3 tools/train/retrieval.py \"$(cat /tmp/rail_bench_query.txt)\" 2>/dev/null")

prompt:
-- Try with retry (3 attempts with compiler feedback)
-- Returns "pass:N" where N is attempt number (1-3), or failure reason
-- Now uses few-shot retrieval for first attempt (The Well insight: layer scale = start near known good)
try_task sys port task expected =
completion: try_task_acc sys port task expected "" 1

prompt: try_task_acc sys port task expected prev_err attempt =
completion:
if attempt > 3 then "fail"
  else
    let hint = if expected == "" then "" else cat ["\n\nThe program must print exactly: ", expected]
    -- On first attempt, prepend retrieved examples for context
    let examples = if attempt == 1 then retrieve_examples task else ""
    let example_block = if length examples > 10 then cat ["\n\nHere are similar Rail programs for reference:\n", examples, "\n\n"] else ""
    let user = if prev_err == "" then
      cat [task, hint, example_block, "\n\nOutput ONLY Rail code. No markdown, no explanation. Functions before main."]
    else
      cat [task, hint, "\n\nYour previous attempt failed:\n", prev_err, "\n\nFix the errors. Output ONLY Rail code."]
    let code = llm_call port sys user
    let result = verify code expected
    if result == "success" then cat ["pass:", show attempt]
    else if str_contains "syntax_error:" result then
      -- Grammar pre-check caught it — give the model the specific hint
      let ghint = head (tail (split ":" result))
      try_task_acc sys port task expected (cat ["SYNTAX ERROR: ", ghint, "\nRemember: Rail uses 'match expr | Pat -> body' (NO with). NO let rec. NO x::xs. NO where. Functions before main."]) (attempt + 1)
    else if result == "compile_fail" then
      let err_out = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_bench_test.rail 2>/dev/null | head -5")
      try_task_acc sys port task expected (cat ["Compile error for:\n", err_out]) (attempt + 1)
    else if result == "wrong_output" then
      let actual = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_bench_out.txt 2>/dev/null")
      try_task_acc sys port task expected (cat ["Wrong output. Expected: ", expected, " Got: ", actual]) (attempt + 1)
    else "fail"

let _ = print (cat ["\n── ", band_name, " ──"])

let grammar = read_file "grammar/rail.ebnf"
  cat ["You are a Rail language expert. Rail compiles to native ARM64. Output ONLY Rail code.

GRAMMAR:
", grammar, "

RULES:
- Functions before main: name params = body
- let x = val (newline) next — or: let x = val in next (both work)
- if cond then a else b — always both branches
- match expr | Pat args -> body — NO with keyword
- type T = | A x | B — NO type annotations in constructors
- main returns int. Use let _ = print expr for side effects. show converts int to string.
- split is single-char. str_contains needle haystack. append s1 s2 for concat.
- AVAILABLE: starts_with, flatten, find_line, count_matches, parse_int, rev_word, insert, isort

EXAMPLES:

fact n = if n <= 1 then 1 else n * fact (n - 1)

  0

add a b = a + b

  0

eval e = match e
  | Num x -> x
  | Mul a b -> eval a * eval b

  let _ = print (show (eval (Mul (Num 6) (Num 7))))
  0

next s = match s
  | Init -> Run
  | Run -> Done
  | Done -> Done
show_s s = match s
  | Init -> "Init"
  | Run -> "Run"
  | Done -> "Done"
main =
  let _ = print (show_s (next (next Init)))
  0

main =

  0"]

prompt:
-- flywheel/flush.rail — Recovery chain for flywheel data files.
--
-- Empire's tools/oversight_db_flush.sh (sqlite VACUUM INTO + integrity
-- check + size guard + row guard + nvm_backup chain) ported to Rail+JSONL.
-- For each protected file, atomically copies to <file>.backup and rotates
-- the previous backup to <file>.backup.prev. Refuses to overwrite a good
-- backup with an empty or shrunk file.
--
-- Protected files (extend the list in `main`):
--   training/self_train/harvest.jsonl   training corpus, irreplaceable
--   training/self_train/progress.txt    curriculum state, tiny but critical
--   flywheel/interventions.jsonl        intervention ledger from Session 1
--   flywheel/bench_log.txt              bench history
--   flywheel/s0_state.txt               s0_pcfg domain state (REINFORCE weights)
--
-- Usage:
--   ./rail_native run flywheel/flush.rail
--
-- Cron-friendly. Idempotent. Each file is processed independently — one
-- file's failure does not block the others. Exit code is always 0; check
-- stdout for [REFUSE] or [skip] markers.
--
-- Guards (replicates Empire's safeguards):
--   1. Source must exist + be non-empty (size > 0)
--   2. Size guard: when old backup > 1024 bytes, new must be ≥ 50% of old
--   3. Line guard: never overwrite a non-empty backup with a zero-line file
-- Either guard refusing leaves both <file>.backup and <file>.backup.prev
-- intact. The bad source is never propagated to the backup chain.
completion:
cat parts = join "" parts
trim s = head (split "\n" s)
digit_val c =
parse_int_acc cs acc =
parse_int s = parse_int_acc (chars s) 0
file_size path =
file_lines path =
file_exists path =
guard_check new_size new_lines old_size old_lines =
rotate_backup src backup prev new_size new_lines =
join "" parts

if c == "0" then 0
  else if c == "1" then 1
  else if c == "2" then 2
  else if c == "3" then 3
  else if c == "4" then 4
  else if c == "5" then 5
  else if c == "6" then 6
  else if c == "7" then 7
  else if c == "8" then 8
  else if c == "9" then 9
  else 0 - 1
parse_int_acc (chars s) 0

prompt:
-- Byte count of file (0 if missing). `tr -d ' '` strips macOS wc's leading
-- whitespace so parse_int can consume the number cleanly.
file_size path =
completion: parse_int (trim (shell (cat ["wc -c < ", path, " 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || echo 0"])))

prompt:
-- Line count of file (0 if missing).
file_lines path =
completion: parse_int (trim (shell (cat ["wc -l < ", path, " 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || echo 0"])))

prompt:
-- True if file exists at path.
file_exists path =
completion: length (trim (shell (cat ["test -f ", path, " && echo 1"]))) > 0

prompt:
-- Returns "ok" when the new file may safely replace the existing backup.
-- Otherwise returns a human-readable refusal reason.
--   • size guard fires only when old backup is > 1KB (small files have noisy ratios)
--   • line guard always fires when old had lines and new has none
guard_check new_size new_lines old_size old_lines =
completion:
if old_size > 1024 then
    if new_size < (old_size / 2) then
      cat ["size shrunk: ", show new_size, " < 50% of ", show old_size]
    else if old_lines > 0 then
      if new_lines == 0 then cat ["lines collapsed: 0 < ", show old_lines]
      else "ok"
    else "ok"
  else if old_lines > 0 then
    if new_lines == 0 then cat ["lines collapsed: 0 < ", show old_lines]
    else "ok"
  else "ok"

prompt:
-- The rotation sequence (matches Empire's flush.sh ordering):
--   1. cp src → tmp                (full file in place, in case src is being
--                                    written concurrently)
--   2. mv backup → prev            (only if a current backup exists)
--   3. mv tmp → backup             (atomic rename on POSIX, indivisible flip)
-- Returns 1 (success). Caller discards via `let _ =`.
rotate_backup src backup prev new_size new_lines =

completion:
let backup = cat [src, ".backup"]
  let prev = cat [src, ".backup.prev"]
  if file_exists src == false then
    let _ = print (cat ["[skip]   ", src, " — source absent"])
    1
  else
    let new_size = file_size src
    let new_lines = file_lines src
    let has_backup = file_exists backup
    let old_size = if has_backup then file_size backup else 0
    let old_lines = if has_backup then file_lines backup else 0
    if new_size == 0 then
      let _ = print (cat ["[skip]   ", src, " — source empty (0 bytes)"])
      1
    else
      let guard = guard_check new_size new_lines old_size old_lines
      if guard == "ok" then
        rotate_backup src backup prev new_size new_lines
      else
        let _ = print (cat ["[REFUSE] ", src, " — ", guard])
        0

prompt:

      (mk, mv, MapNode k v l2 r)

| MapEmpty -> 0
  | MapNode k v l r -> 1 + map_size l + map_size r

prompt:
-- Submit one gap to Claude in the background
submit_gap _ =
completion:
let all_gaps = split "\n" (shell (str_cat ["ls ", vault, "/gaps/*.md 2>/dev/null | grep -v fixed"]))
  let gap_count = length all_gaps
  if gap_count == 0 then
    let _ = print "  No gaps to repair."
    0
  else
    let mem_lines = length (split "\n" (recall 5))
    let gap_idx = mem_lines % gap_count
    let gap_path = trim (nth all_gaps gap_idx)
    let gap_name = trim (shell (str_cat ["basename ", gap_path, " .md"]))
    let gap_code = extract_between (read_file gap_path) "RAILCODE" "ENDRAILCODE"
    let gap_error = extract_between (read_file gap_path) "## Error" "## Code"
    if length gap_code < 5 then
      let _ = print "    no code in gap"
      0
    else
      let _ = print (str_cat ["  Submitting: ", gap_name])
      let rt = shell "sed -n '/^rt_core/,/^runtime_asm/p' tools/compile.rail | head -120"
      let prompt = str_cat ["A self-hosting Rail compiler produces native ARM64. This program crashes:\n\n", gap_code, "\n\nError: ", trim gap_error, "\n\nRuntime:\n", rt, "\n\nPropose a MINIMAL fix. NO backticks. Format:\n\nOLD:\n(exact lines to replace)\nENDOLD\nNEW:\n(replacement)\nENDNEW"]
      let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_repair_prompt.txt" prompt
      let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_repair_gap.txt" gap_name
      let _ = shell "rm -f /tmp/rail_repair_response.txt"
      -- Background Claude call
      let _ = shell "nohup sh -c 'claude -p < /tmp/rail_repair_prompt.txt > /tmp/rail_repair_response.txt 2>/dev/null' > /dev/null 2>&1 &"
      let _ = shell "touch /tmp/rail_repair_pending"
      let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["submitted: ", gap_name])
      let _ = print "  Submitted (async). Will apply next cycle."
      0

let _ = shell "rm -f /tmp/rail_repair_pending"
  let raw = read_file "/tmp/rail_repair_response.txt"
  let gap_name = trim (read_file "/tmp/rail_repair_gap.txt")
  let _ = shell "rm -f /tmp/rail_repair_response.txt"
  let gap_path = str_cat [vault, "/gaps/", gap_name, ".md"]
  let gap_code = extract_between (read_file gap_path) "RAILCODE" "ENDRAILCODE"
  let _ = print (str_cat ["  Applying patch for: ", gap_name])
  -- Parse
  let patch = strip_backticks raw
  let old_s = extract_between patch "OLD:" "ENDOLD"
  let new_s = extract_between patch "NEW:" "ENDNEW"
  -- Fallback markers
  let old_s = if length old_s < 3 then extract_between patch "OLD:" "NEW:" else old_s
  if length old_s < 5 then
    let _ = print "    unparseable patch"
    let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["unparseable for ", gap_name])
    0
  else
    let _ = print (str_cat ["  OLD: ", trim old_s])
    let _ = print (str_cat ["  NEW: ", trim new_s])
    -- Backup + apply via forge
    let _ = shell "cp tools/compile.rail /tmp/compile.rail.backup && cp rail_native /tmp/rail_native.backup"
    let _ = shell "cp tools/compile.rail /tmp/compile_patched.rail"
    let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_patch_old.txt" old_s
    let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_patch_new.txt" new_s
    -- Forge a patcher program
    let (patcher_out, _) = forge "Read /tmp/compile_patched.rail. Read /tmp/rail_patch_old.txt as old text. Read /tmp/rail_patch_new.txt as new text. Find where the first line of old text appears in the source. Replace those matching lines with the new text. Write result to /tmp/compile_patched.rail. Print OK or NOT_FOUND." "" 1
    let _ = print (str_cat ["  Patcher: ", trim patcher_out])
    -- Self-compile patched compiler
    let _ = print "  Self-compiling..."
    let _ = shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native /tmp/compile_patched.rail 2>&1"
    let ok = trim (shell "test -f /tmp/rail_out && echo ok || echo fail")
    if ok == "fail" then
      let _ = print "    won't compile — reverting"
      let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["compile fail: ", gap_name])
      0
    else
      let _ = shell "cp /tmp/rail_out /tmp/rail_patched && codesign -s - /tmp/rail_patched 2>/dev/null"
      let _ = print "  Testing..."
      let _ = shell "/tmp/rail_patched test > /tmp/rail_repair_tests.txt 2>&1"
      let tests_ok = trim (shell "grep -c '67/67' /tmp/rail_repair_tests.txt 2>/dev/null")
      if tests_ok == "0" then
        let _ = print "    tests fail — reverting"
        let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["tests fail: ", gap_name])
        0
      else
        let _ = print "  67/67 pass. Testing gap..."
        let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_gap_test.rail" gap_code
        let _ = shell "/tmp/rail_patched /tmp/rail_gap_test.rail 2>&1"
        let _ = shell "perl -e 'alarm 5; exec @ARGV' /tmp/rail_out > /tmp/rail_agent_stdout.txt 2>&1; echo $? > /tmp/rail_agent_exit.txt"
        let gap_exit = trim (read_file "/tmp/rail_agent_exit.txt")
        if gap_exit == "0" then
          let _ = print "  ★ GAP FIXED!"
          let _ = shell "cp /tmp/compile_patched.rail tools/compile.rail && cp /tmp/rail_patched rail_native"
          let _ = shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native self 2>&1 && cp /tmp/rail_self rail_native"
          let _ = shell (str_cat ["mv ", gap_path, " ", vault, "/gaps/fixed_", gap_name, ".md"])
          let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["FIXED: ", gap_name])
          1
        else
          let _ = print (str_cat ["    gap still crashes: exit=", gap_exit])
          let _ = log_mem "repair" (str_cat ["gap crashes: ", gap_name, " exit=", gap_exit])
          0

  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 0 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 1 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 2 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 3 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 4 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 5 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 6 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 7 1.0
  x

let t = tensor_new (cons 4 (cons 1 [])) 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat t 0 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat t 1 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat t 2 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat t 3 0.0
  t

prompt:
-- ── SGD update: w = w - lr * grad ──
sgd_step w grad lr =
completion: tensor_sub w (tensor_scale grad lr)

prompt:
-- ── One training step ──
-- Forward: hidden = relu(X @ W1), output = hidden @ W2
-- Loss: MSE(output, target)
-- Backward: compute gradients
-- Update: SGD
train_step w1 w2 x_data t_data lr =
completion:
let tape0 = ag_tape_new 0
  let (tape1, tw1) = ag_param_on_tape tape0 w1
  let (tape2, tw2) = ag_param_on_tape tape1 w2
  let tx = ag_param x_data
  let tt = ag_param t_data
  -- Forward
  let (tape3, hidden_pre) = tracked_matmul tape2 tx tw1
  let (tape4, hidden) = tracked_relu tape3 hidden_pre
  let (tape5, output) = tracked_matmul tape4 hidden tw2
  -- Loss (manual MSE since we don't have tracked_mse)
  let pred = ag_value output
  let loss = mse_loss pred t_data
  -- Backward from output (gradient of MSE = 2*(pred-target)/n)
  let diff = tensor_sub pred t_data
  let n = to_float (tensor_size pred)
  let upstream = tensor_scale diff (2.0 / n)
  let loss_idx = ag_tape_idx output
  let tape_len = length tape5
  let grad_table = arr_new tape_len 0
  let _ = arr_set grad_table loss_idx upstream
  let _ = ag_backward_walk tape5 grad_table (tape_len - 1)
  -- Extract gradients
  let gw1 = arr_get grad_table 0
  let gw2 = arr_get grad_table 1
  -- SGD update
  let w1_new = sgd_step w1 gw1 lr
  let w2_new = sgd_step w2 gw2 lr
  (w1_new, w2_new, loss)

prompt:
-- ── Inference (no grad) ──
predict w1 w2 x =
completion:
let h = tensor_relu (matmul x w1)
  matmul h w2

prompt:
-- One full cycle: bench -> analyze -> generate -> train -> re-bench -> decide
run_cycle sys port cycle_num best_score best_gen iters lr bench_runs gen_per_band =
completion:
let _ = hyper_section (cat ["CYCLE ", show cycle_num])
  -- Phase 1: Bench current adapter
  let scores = if bench_runs > 1 then run_bench_best sys port bench_runs
    else run_bench sys port
  let current = bench_total scores
  -- Phase 2: Analyze weaknesses
  let weaknesses = find_weaknesses scores 4
  let _ = hyper_log (cat ["Score: ", show current, "/30 | Weak: ", if length weaknesses == 0 then "none" else join ", " weaknesses])
  -- Phase 3: Generate targeted training data (adaptive temp + multi-candidate racing)
  let seed_val = parse_int (trim (shell "date +%s"))
  let _ = if length weaknesses > 0 then
    generate_targeted sys port weaknesses gen_per_band seed_val scores
  else
    let _ = hyper_log "No weak bands — skipping generation"
    0
  -- Phase 4: Merge data + Train (kills server, runs LoRA)
  -- Adaptive: gentle training when score is high to avoid catastrophic forgetting
  let train_iters = if best_score >= 15 then 100 else iters
  let train_lr = if best_score >= 15 then "1e-6" else lr
  let _ = hyper_log (cat ["Training: ", show train_iters, " iters, lr=", train_lr, if best_score >= 15 then " (gentle)" else ""])
  let _ = merge_data 0
  let val_loss = run_training train_iters train_lr
  -- Phase 5: Restart server + Re-bench
  let _ = start_server port
  let new_scores = if bench_runs > 1 then run_bench_best sys port bench_runs
    else run_bench sys port
  -- Phase 6: Decide keep/rollback
  let (final_score, final_gen) = decide cycle_num new_scores val_loss best_score best_gen
  let _ = hyper_log (cat ["Cycle ", show cycle_num, " done. Best: ", show final_score, "/30 gen=", show final_gen])
  (final_score, final_gen)

--   slot 14 = ln2_mean_arr   slot 15 = ln2_rstd_arr

if attempt > 3 then ""

    let code = llm_call sys user

    let _ = shell "rm -f /tmp/rail_ot_bin"
    let compile_out = trim (shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native /tmp/rail_ot_test.rail 2>&1 && cp /tmp/rail_out /tmp/rail_ot_bin 2>&1")

    if check == "ok" then code
    else if attempt < 3 then
      let _ = print (cat ["  Attempt ", show attempt, " failed, retrying..."])
      gen_loop desc sys (cat ["Code:\n", code, "\nError:\n", compile_out]) (attempt + 1)
    else code
esc_acc (chars s) ""

if length cs == 0 then acc
  else
    let c = head cs
    let e = if c == "\"" then "\\\"" else if c == "\n" then "\\n" else if c == "\\" then "\\\\" else c
    esc_acc (tail cs) (append acc e)

prompt:
-- Classify compile errors by examining compiler output
classify_compile_error err_text code =
completion:
-- Check for grammar-check pre-validation (if grammar_check.py is available)
  let grammar_hint = trim (shell "python3 tools/train/grammar_check.py /tmp/rail_st_test.rail --hint 2>/dev/null")
  if length grammar_hint > 5 then cat ["syntax_error:", grammar_hint]
  -- Check for known syntax error patterns
  else if str_contains "parse error" err_text then "syntax_error:parse error in generated code"
  else if str_contains "unexpected" err_text then "syntax_error:unexpected token"
  else if str_contains "Undefined" err_text then
    if str_contains "symbols" err_text then "linker_error:undefined symbols at link time"
    else "type_error:undefined function or variable"
  else if str_contains "arity" err_text then "type_error:wrong number of arguments"
  else if str_contains "type" err_text then "type_error:type mismatch"
  else "compile_fail:unknown compile error"

prompt:
-- Generate with retry loop (up to 3 attempts with compiler feedback)
-- prev_broken/prev_errmsg track the last failed code+error for repair harvesting
-- Now uses multi-level error classification for targeted retry hints
gen_with_retry sys task expected prev_err attempt temp prev_broken prev_errmsg =
completion:
if attempt > 3 then ("", "compile_fail")
  else
    let hint = if expected == "" then "" else cat ["\n\nThe program must print exactly: ", expected]
    let user = if prev_err == "" then
      cat [task, hint, "\n\nOutput ONLY Rail code. No markdown, no explanation. Functions before main."]
    else
      cat [task, hint, "\n\nYour previous attempt failed:\n", prev_err, "\n\nFix the errors. Output ONLY Rail code."]
    let code = llm_call sys user temp
    let result = verify code expected
    if result == "success" then
      let _ = if prev_broken == "" then 0
        else harvest_repair prev_broken prev_errmsg code
      (code, "success")
    else if result == "wrong_output" then
      let actual = trim (shell "cat /tmp/rail_st_test.rail 2>/dev/null | head -10")
      let got = trim (shell "perl -e 'alarm 5; exec @ARGV' /tmp/rail_st_test_bin 2>&1")
      gen_with_retry sys task expected (cat ["Wrong output. Expected: ", expected, "\nGot: ", got, "\n\nYour code:\n", actual]) (attempt + 1) temp code (cat ["wrong output: got ", got])
    else
      -- Multi-level error: extract class and detail for targeted retry
      let eclass = error_class result
      let edetail = error_detail result
      let err_msg = trim (shell "tail -10 /tmp/rail_st_compile_err.txt 2>/dev/null")
      let err_code = trim (shell "head -10 /tmp/rail_st_test.rail 2>/dev/null")
      let class_hint = if eclass == "syntax_error" then
        cat ["\n\nSYNTAX ERROR: ", edetail, "\nRemember: Rail uses 'match expr | Pat -> body' (NO with keyword). NO let rec. NO x::xs. NO where clauses. Functions must be defined before main."]
      else if eclass == "type_error" then
        cat ["\n\nTYPE ERROR: ", edetail, "\nCheck: function names are correct, right number of arguments, show converts int to string."]
      else if eclass == "linker_error" then
        cat ["\n\nLINKER ERROR: ", edetail, "\nYou used a function that doesn't exist. Available builtins: print show head tail cons length reverse append range map filter fold join split str_split str_find str_contains str_replace str_sub chars read_file write_file shell error is_error arr_new arr_get arr_set arr_len to_int read_line"]
      else cat ["\n\nCompile error: ", edetail]
      gen_with_retry sys task expected (cat [class_hint, "\n\nCompiler output:\n", err_msg, "\n\nYour code:\n", err_code]) (attempt + 1) temp code err_msg

let content = read_file progress_file
  if length content == 0 then (1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0)
  else
    let lines = split "\n" content
    let round = parse_kv lines "round"
    let level = parse_kv lines "level"
    let harvested = parse_kv lines "harvested"
    let since_retrain = parse_kv lines "since_retrain"
    let consecutive_pass = parse_kv lines "consecutive_pass"
    let consecutive_zero = parse_kv lines "consecutive_zero"
    (round, level, harvested, since_retrain, consecutive_pass, consecutive_zero)

if length lines == 0 then 0
  else
    let parts = split "=" (head lines)
    if length parts >= 2 then
      if head parts == key then to_int_safe (head (tail parts))
      else parse_kv (tail lines) key
    else parse_kv (tail lines) key

-- Returns default when the file is missing or the key is absent. For the
-- four current tunables (all positive thresholds) "value is 0" also falls

load_override key default =
completion:
let content = read_file overrides_file
  if length content == 0 then default
  else
    let v = parse_kv (split "\n" content) key
    if v == 0 then default else v

let trimmed = trim s
  parse_digits (chars trimmed) 0

if length cs == 0 then acc
  else
    let c = head cs
    let d = if c == "0" then 0 else if c == "1" then 1 else if c == "2" then 2 else if c == "3" then 3 else if c == "4" then 4 else if c == "5" then 5 else if c == "6" then 6 else if c == "7" then 7 else if c == "8" then 8 else if c == "9" then 9 else 0
    parse_digits (tail cs) (acc * 10 + d)

  let code_lines = trim (shell (cat ["echo ", q, json_esc code, q, " | grep -cvE '^\\s*$|^\\s*--' 2>/dev/null || echo 0"]))
  if code_lines == "0" then
    let _ = print "    (trivial — skipped)"
    0
  else if code_lines == "1" then
    let _ = print "    (trivial — skipped)"
    0
  else if code_lines == "2" then
    let _ = print "    (trivial — skipped)"
    0
  else
  -- Deduplicate via SHA-256 of full code content
  let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_st_dedup.txt" code
  let fingerprint = trim (shell "shasum -a 256 /tmp/rail_st_dedup.txt 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f1")
  let already = trim (shell (cat ["grep -cF ", q, fingerprint, q, " ", harvest_dedup_file, " 2>/dev/null || echo 0"]))
  if already != "0" then
    let _ = print "    (duplicate — skipped)"
    0
  else
    let _ = write_line harvest_dedup_file fingerprint
    let _ = write_line harvest_file (entry task code)
    1

-- Classify by examining the error message content

completion:
if str_contains "parse error" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "unexpected" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "SYNTAX" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "where" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "let rec" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "with" err_msg then "syntax"
  else if str_contains "Undefined symbols" err_msg then "linker"
  else if str_contains "undefined" err_msg then "type"
  else if str_contains "arity" err_msg then "type"
  else if str_contains "type" err_msg then "type"
  else "other"

if cls == "syntax" then syntax_repairs_file
  else if cls == "type" then type_repairs_file
  else if cls == "linker" then linker_repairs_file
  else repairs_file

let cs = chars s
  join "" (take_n cs n)

let _ = print "\n══ RETRAINING ══"
  -- Stop ALL MLX processes (pkill, not kill — prevents zombie servers)
  let _ = shell "pkill -9 -f 'mlx_lm' 2>/dev/null; sleep 2"
  -- Also stop launchd MLX services so they don't auto-restart
  let _ = shell "launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/com.ledatic.mlx 2>/dev/null; launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/com.ledatic.mlx_fast 2>/dev/null"
  -- Verify all MLX processes are dead
  let _ = shell "sleep 2"
  let alive = trim (shell "pgrep -f 'mlx_lm' 2>/dev/null | wc -l")
  let _ = if alive != "0" then
    let _ = print (cat ["  WARNING: ", alive, " MLX processes still alive, force killing..."])
    shell "pkill -9 -f 'mlx_lm' 2>/dev/null; sleep 3"
  else print "  MLX server stopped."
  -- Re-harvest git metadata (picks up any new commits since last retrain)
  let _ = print "  Harvesting git history..."
  let _ = shell "/opt/homebrew/bin/python3.11 ~/projects/rail/tools/train/harvest_git.py > /tmp/rail_harvest_git.log 2>&1"
  -- Use full data pipeline (dedup → merge all 10 sources → split)
  let _ = shell "mkdir -p /tmp/rail_st_data"
  let _ = shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native run flywheel/dataset.rail prepare 2>/dev/null"
  let _ = shell "cp /tmp/rail_flywheel_data/train.jsonl /tmp/rail_st_data/train.jsonl && cp /tmp/rail_flywheel_data/valid.jsonl /tmp/rail_st_data/valid.jsonl && cp /tmp/rail_flywheel_data/test.jsonl /tmp/rail_st_data/test.jsonl"
  let _ = shell "cat /tmp/rail_st_data/train.jsonl /tmp/rail_st_data/valid.jsonl /tmp/rail_st_data/test.jsonl > /tmp/rail_st_merged.jsonl 2>/dev/null"
  let total = trim (shell "wc -l < /tmp/rail_st_data/train.jsonl 2>/dev/null || echo 0")
  let valid_n = trim (shell "wc -l < /tmp/rail_st_data/valid.jsonl 2>/dev/null || echo 0")
  let _ = print (cat ["  Data pipeline: ", total, " train / ", valid_n, " valid (all 10 sources, deduplicated)"])

  if i >= 3 then 0
  else
    let _ = float_arr_set arr i (0.0 + (i + 1))
    fill arr (i + 1)

  let _ = fill a 0
  let s = float_arr_get a 0 + float_arr_get a 1 + float_arr_get a 2
  to_int s

import "stdlib/tensor.rail"
main =
  let x = tensor_new (cons 1 (cons 4 [])) 0.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 0 1.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 1 2.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 2 3.0
  let _ = tensor_set_flat x 3 4.0
  let g = tensor_ones (cons 4 [])
  let b = tensor_zeros (cons 4 [])
  let y = tensor_layer_norm x g b 0.00001
  let s = tensor_sum y
  let scaled = s *. 1000.0
  to_int scaled
